old_claim.note: X-Ray Imaging provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Operational hospital information systems require electronic computing infrastructure; HELP was implemented on a Tandem computer system with CPUs, disk storage, and communication ports.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract description of HELP implementation on Tandem non-stop computer system and its CPU, disk, and communication-port architecture.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The HELP article describes a data-driven hospital and medical information system implemented on a Tandem computer system with CPUs, storage, communication ports, and multiple software subsystems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports computers as a direct HIS infrastructure dependency and rejects X-ray imaging as the direct prerequisite because X-ray appears only as one application area served by the system.

ontology_before: Hospital information systems were modeled as enabled by an imaging modality, which confused one clinical application with the information-system platform.
ontology_after: Hospital information systems are modeled as computer-based software infrastructure that can integrate imaging, laboratory, pharmacy, records, and other workflows.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital_information_systems no longer directly depends on x_ray_imaging and now requires computers_early.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to a radiology information system rather than a broad hospital information system.
A source showed hospital information systems can be implemented without electronic computing infrastructure.
The graph reintroduced x_ray_imaging as a direct prerequisite for the broad HIS node.
short_reason: X-ray is an application HIS can serve; computers are the platform HIS requires.
